nodes.label: princess | put | shoe
nodes.sentence: One pleasant evening, a young princess put on her bonnet and wooden shoes and went for a walk alone in the forest.

nodes.label: she | sit | ∅
nodes.sentence: When she came to a cool spring of water in the middle of the woods, she sat down to rest for a while.

nodes.label: she | hold | toy
nodes.sentence: She was holding her favorite toy, a golden ball, which she liked to toss into the air and catch again.

nodes.label: she | throw | it
nodes.sentence: After a while, she threw it up so high that she missed catching it, and the ball bounced away and rolled on the ground until it finally fell into the spring.

nodes.label: princess | look | water
nodes.sentence: The princess looked into the deep water but couldn’t see the bottom.

nodes.label: she | begin | ∅
nodes.sentence: She began to cry and said, “Oh no!

nodes.label: frog | pop | head
nodes.sentence:
If only I could get my ball back, I would give all my fine clothes, jewels, and everything I own.”

While she was speaking, a frog popped its head out of the water and asked, “Princess, why are you crying so badly?”

nodes.label: she | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: She replied, “What can you do for me, you ugly frog?

nodes.label: ball | fall | spring
nodes.sentence: My golden ball fell into the spring.”

nodes.label: frog | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: The frog said, “I don’t want your jewels or fine clothes.

nodes.label: i | get | ball
nodes.sentence: But if you love me, let me live with you, eat from your golden plate, and sleep on your bed, I will get your ball back.”

nodes.label: ∅ | think | herself
nodes.sentence: The princess thought to herself, “What nonsense!

nodes.label: frog | leave | spring
nodes.sentence: This silly frog can’t even leave the spring.

nodes.label: i | promise | he
nodes.sentence: But if he can get my ball, I’ll promise him whatever he wants.”

nodes.label: she | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: So she said, “Alright, if you bring me my ball, I’ll do as you ask.”

nodes.label: frog | dive | underwater
nodes.sentence: The frog then dived deep underwater and soon resurfaced with the ball in his mouth, tossing it onto the spring’s edge.

nodes.label: princess | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The princess was so happy to have her ball again that she forgot all about the frog and ran home as fast as she could.

nodes.label: frog | call | wait
nodes.sentence: The frog called after her, “Wait, princess, take me with you as you promised!”

nodes.label: she | stop | ∅
nodes.sentence: But she didn’t stop.

nodes.label: she | hear | noise
nodes.sentence: The next day, just as the princess sat down to dinner, she heard a strange noise—tap, tap—plash, plash—like something was climbing the marble stairs.

nodes.label: ∅ | open | it
nodes.sentence:
Soon there was a gentle knock on the door and a small voice called:

“Open the door, my dear princess, 
Open it to your true love here!

nodes.label: ∅ | remember | word
nodes.sentence:
Remember the words that you and I said 
By the cool fountain in the green forest shade.”

nodes.label: princess | run | door
nodes.sentence: The princess ran to the door and opened it, and there was the frog she had forgotten.

nodes.label: she | shut | door
nodes.sentence: Frightened, she quickly shut the door and went back to her seat.

nodes.label: father | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: Her father, the king, asked what was wrong.

nodes.label: she | say | one
nodes.sentence: “There’s a nasty frog at the door,” she said, “the one who got my ball out of the spring this morning.

nodes.label: i | tell | he
nodes.sentence: I told him he could live with me, thinking he couldn’t leave the spring—but now he’s at the door wanting to come in.”

nodes.label: frog | knock | ∅
nodes.sentence:
As she spoke, the frog knocked again and said:

“Open the door, my dear princess, 
Open it to your true love here!

nodes.label: ∅ | remember | word
nodes.sentence:
Remember the words that you and I said 
By the cool fountain in the green forest shade.”

nodes.label: king | tell | daughter
nodes.sentence: The king told his daughter, “Since you gave your word, you must keep it.

nodes.label: ∅ | go | ∅
nodes.sentence: Go and let him in.”

nodes.label: she | do | ∅
nodes.sentence: Reluctantly, she did.

nodes.label: frog | hop | ∅
nodes.sentence: The frog hopped inside and then hopped all around the room until he reached the table where the princess sat.

nodes.label: frog | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Please lift me onto a chair,” the frog said, “and let me sit beside you.”

nodes.label: she | do | ∅
nodes.sentence: She did so, and he said, “Move your plate closer to me so I can eat.”

nodes.label: i | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: She did, and when he had finished, he said, “Now I am tired.

nodes.label: ∅ | carry | i
nodes.sentence: Carry me upstairs and put me to bed.”

nodes.label: princess | carry | he
nodes.sentence: Though unwilling, the princess carried him to her bed, placed him on her pillow, and he slept all night.

nodes.label: he | jump | dawn
nodes.sentence: At dawn, he jumped up, hopped downstairs, and left the house.

nodes.label: she | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: But she was wrong.

nodes.label: she | hear | tapping
nodes.sentence: That very night, she heard the same tapping at the door.

nodes.label: frog | come | ∅
nodes.sentence:
The frog came again and said:

“Open the door, my dear princess, 
Open it to your true love here!

nodes.label: ∅ | remember | word
nodes.sentence:
Remember the words that you and I said 
By the cool fountain in the green forest shade.”

nodes.label: frog | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the princess opened the door, the frog came in and slept on her pillow as before until morning.

nodes.label: he | do | thing
nodes.sentence: The next two nights he did the same thing.

nodes.label: she | be | morning
nodes.sentence: But on the morning after the third night, she was amazed to find, instead of the frog, a handsome prince looking at her with the most beautiful eyes she had ever seen, standing at the head of her bed.

nodes.label: he | tell | she
nodes.sentence: He told her that an evil fairy had enchanted him, turning him into a frog.

nodes.label: he | have | ∅
nodes.sentence: He had to remain so until a princess rescued him from the spring, let him eat from her plate, and sleep in her bed for three nights.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “You have broken this cruel spell,” he said, “and now I wish only to take you to my father’s kingdom, marry you, and love you forever.”

nodes.label: princess | agree | ∅
nodes.sentence: The young princess eagerly agreed.

nodes.label: coach | arrive | ∅
nodes.sentence: Just then, a splendid coach arrived, pulled by eight magnificent horses adorned with feather plumes and golden harnesses.

nodes.label: ∅ | ride | servant
nodes.sentence: Behind the coach rode the prince’s loyal servant, Heinrich, who had grieved bitterly over his master’s curse.

nodes.label: they | say | king
nodes.sentence: They said farewell to the king, climbed into the coach, and with joy and celebration, journeyed to the prince’s kingdom.

nodes.label: they | live | year
nodes.sentence: There, they lived happily for many years.
